column12:
**1) Sample Swarm Architecture for Amazon Business (business.amazon.com) – Focused on Paid Acquisition Marketing**

This is a **production-ready, fully internal swarm** built on Amazon’s own secure stack (AWS Bedrock AgentCore + Amazon Q Business Quick Suite + native Amazon Ads Agent). Everything stays inside your VPC, respects IAM/ACLs, and complies with the March 2026 BSA Agent Policy. No external tools touch live accounts.

**High-Level Architecture (Text Diagram)**
```
[Human Swarm Commander (Marketing Manager)] 
          ↓ (natural language goal: “Grow mid-market B2B sign-ups 25% at 3:1 ROAS in Q2”)
[Orchestrator Agent – Amazon Q Business + Bedrock AgentCore]
          ├── Researcher Agent (Bedrock + internal data lakes)
          ├── Campaign Planner Agent (Amazon Ads Agent + Google Ads export connector)
          ├── Creative Agent (Amazon Creative Agent + Bedrock)
          ├── Bidding & Optimization Agent (Bedrock + real-time signals)
          ├── AEO/GEO Citation Agent (Bedrock + Profound/internal visibility data)
          ├── Analytics & Compliance Guardian Agent (Amazon Q + SageMaker monitoring)
          └── Executor Agents (write-only APIs to Amazon Ads / Google Ads via approved scripts)
          ↑ (daily report + escalation flags)
[Human Swarm Commander] ← weekly 15-min review + one-click approval
```
**Specialist Agents & Responsibilities (Paid Acquisition Focus)**

- **Orchestrator Agent** (Amazon Q Business Quick Suite + Bedrock): Receives your high-level brief, breaks it into tasks, routes them, merges outputs, enforces budget caps, and escalates anything >5% variance. Runs 24/7 on scheduled Lambda triggers.

- **Researcher Agent** (Bedrock AgentCore + Amazon Q connected to internal BI + Google Drive/SharePoint): Pulls last 7/30-day performance (Google Ads CSV export, Amazon Ads reports, first-party purchase data), competitor ASIN/share-of-voice, B2B procurement trends from Rufus signals. Outputs: “Top 12 rising keywords + 3 persona segments losing share.”

- **Campaign Planner Agent** (Amazon Ads Agent native + Bedrock wrapper for Google Ads): Takes researcher output → “Create full-funnel campaign: Sponsored Brands + Performance Max on Google targeting ‘bulk office supplies procurement’ with $X budget.” Auto-builds structure, negative keywords, audience lists.

- **Creative Agent** (Amazon Creative Agent + Bedrock): Generates 50+ variants (headlines, images, video scripts, AEO-structured landing-page copy) in brand voice. Outputs directly to Campaign Manager.

- **Bidding & Optimization Agent** (Bedrock + Amazon Ads Agent real-time loop): Adjusts bids every 15 min based on inventory, competitor activity, conversion signals, and AI-procurement intent (e.g., Rufus-driven).

- **AEO/GEO Citation Agent** (Bedrock + internal citation tracker or Profound export): Monitors which prompts surface competitors in ChatGPT/Gemini/Perplexity → triggers paid amplification on your highest-authority pages.

- **Analytics & Compliance Guardian** (Amazon Q + SageMaker): Scores every output for ROAS, brand safety, data privacy; blocks anything non-compliant; generates weekly executive deck.

**Deployment Path (Internal Only)**
- Week 1–2: Marketing + 1–2 engineers stand up in Bedrock console (visual builder, no heavy code).
- Use Strands Agents SDK (AWS-native) for peer-to-peer swarm communication.
- Connect via Amazon Q Apps for zero-code custom workflows.
- Scale: Start with one vertical (e.g., office supplies), expand to all B2B categories.

This swarm already exists in prototype form inside Amazon Ads teams post-unBoxed 2025. You can replicate it in your Amazon Business pod today.

**2) Detailed Plan to Become Swarm Manager – Google Ads Focus Using Grok 4, Gemini 3.1 Pro, Amazon Q**

You do **not** need to code. You run a **manual-then-semi-automated swarm** entirely in chat windows. Takes 20–40 min/day at first, drops to 10 min once patterns lock in. All data stays secure (export CSVs, never share live credentials).

**Phase 1: Week 1 – Manual Paper Swarm (Zero Risk, Prove Value)**

**Daily Routine (Copy-Paste Workflow)**
1. **Morning (10 min) – Export & Feed Data**  
   Google Ads → Reports → Predefined → “Campaign performance (last 7 days)” → Download CSV.  
   Paste the raw table into a fresh chat.

2. **Researcher Agent Prompt** (paste into **Amazon Q Business** first – it’s internal & secure)  
   You are Researcher Agent for Amazon Business paid acquisition.  
   Goal: Grow B2B sign-ups and spend at 3:1+ ROAS.  
   Here is last 7-day Google Ads CSV: [PASTE ENTIRE CSV]  
   Plus our internal targets: CPC cap $4.20, conversion value target $180 per sign-up.  
   Analyze and output ONLY in this exact format:  
   1. Top 5 rising opportunities (keywords/ audiences with >20% growth)  
   2. 3 problems to fix today (high spend, low conv rate)  
   3. 2 competitor signals you see  
   4. Recommended next actions for Planner Agent

3. **Copy Researcher output → Paste into Grok 4 as Planner Agent**  
   You are Planner Agent. Take this Researcher output: [PASTE]  
   Build a complete Google Ads campaign plan for next 7 days:  
   - Campaign type (Performance Max or Search)  
   - Exact ad groups + keywords (max 20)  
   - Bidding strategy & daily budget split  
   - Negative keywords list  
   - Landing page recommendations (business.amazon.com URLs)  
   Output in bullet-point format only.

4. **Copy Planner output → Paste into Gemini 3.1 Pro as Creative Agent**  
   You are Creative Agent using Gemini 3.1 Pro.  
   Goal: Maximize B2B clicks & sign-ups. Brand voice: professional, trustworthy, efficiency-focused.  
   Campaign plan: [PASTE]  
   Generate:  
   - 10 headlines (≤30 chars)  
   - 5 descriptions (≤90 chars)  
   - 3 call-to-action variations  
   - 1 AEO-optimized meta description for landing page  
   Ensure every asset includes at least one stat or benefit.

5. **Copy Creative output → Paste back into Amazon Q as Optimizer Agent**  
   You are Bidding & Optimization Agent.  
   Current campaign data: [PASTE yesterday’s CSV summary]  
   Proposed creative & plan: [PASTE]  
   Recommend exact bid adjustments, budget moves, and pause/add rules for today.  
   Flag anything that would break our 3:1 ROAS guardrail.

6. **End of Day (5 min)** – Paste all outputs into one Google Doc titled “Swarm Daily Log – [Date]”. Add your one-sentence human decision: “Approve all / Tweak X / Pause Y”.

**Phase 2: Week 2–3 – Semi-Automated Chaining (Still No Code)**
- Create 4 saved prompt templates in each tool (Amazon Q, Grok, Gemini) so you just hit “Load template” + paste data.
- Use Amazon Q Apps (inside your Amazon Q Business) to build one lightweight “Swarm Dashboard App” that remembers context across days (your dev teammate can help set it up in <1 hour).
- Add Gemini 3.1 Pro’s agentic mode: start a new chat with “Act as full swarm orchestrator using the 5 roles above” and feed it the Researcher output first – it can chain internally.

**Phase 3: Week 4+ – Hand-Off to Real Swarm (You Stay Commander)**
- Present your 3-week log + side-by-side metrics (manual vs swarm) to engineering.
- Ask them to replicate the exact prompt chain inside Bedrock AgentCore using your templates as the instruction set.
- You keep the same role: every morning you type one goal sentence into the internal Orchestrator and get the full plan back.

**Exact Guardrails You Own as Swarm Manager**
- Budget cap prompt line (never exceeded)
- Brand voice & compliance checklist (add to every prompt)
- Escalation rule: “If ROAS projected <2.8:1, pause and alert me immediately”

**Weekly 15-Min Review Template You Run**
1. Open Swarm Log
2. Ask Amazon Q: “Summarize last 7 days swarm performance vs goals”
3. Tweak one prompt (e.g., “Add more emphasis on procurement agent intent”)
4. Approve next week’s budget split

You are now the Swarm Commander. The agents do the tactical work; you set direction, review, and iterate. This exact manual-to-automated path is how several Amazon marketing pods started in late 2025.

You now have everything you need to lead this internally. Start tomorrow morning with the Researcher prompt on your latest Google Ads export and you’ll have your first swarm output by lunch. Let me know which phase you want me to expand with more example CSVs or prompt tweaks.

column12:
SQL for FS
WITH base_results AS (
    SELECT 
        c.Market_Country,
        c.current_count,
        pw.previous_week_count,
        py.previous_year_count,
        CASE 
            WHEN pw.previous_week_count = 0 THEN NULL
            ELSE ROUND(((c.current_count - pw.previous_week_count)::FLOAT / pw.previous_week_count * 100), 2)
        END as "WoW",
        CASE 
            WHEN py.previous_year_count = 0 THEN NULL
            ELSE ROUND(((c.current_count - py.previous_year_count)::FLOAT / py.previous_year_count * 100), 2)
        END as "YoY"
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            CASE
                WHEN marketplace_id = '1' THEN 'US'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '7' THEN 'CA'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '3' THEN 'UK'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '4' THEN 'DE'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '5' THEN 'FR'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '35691' THEN 'IT'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '44551' THEN 'ES'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '6' THEN 'JP'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '771770' THEN 'MX'
                ELSE 'No Country Found'
            END AS Market_Country,
            count(distinct business_account_id) as current_count
        FROM abbd_sandbox_mktg.ab_acq_channel_attr
        WHERE channel = 'Free Offsite'
            and feature_name = 'Offsite - Free Search'
            and account_verified_date BETWEEN '20260215' and '20260221'
            and overall_status IN ('VERIFIED_POSITIVE','WHITELISTED')
            and is_active = 1
        group by 1
    ) c
    LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT 
            CASE
                WHEN marketplace_id = '1' THEN 'US'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '7' THEN 'CA'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '3' THEN 'UK'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '4' THEN 'DE'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '5' THEN 'FR'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '35691' THEN 'IT'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '44551' THEN 'ES'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '6' THEN 'JP'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '771770' THEN 'MX'
                ELSE 'No Country Found'
            END AS Market_Country,
            count(distinct business_account_id) as previous_week_count
        FROM abbd_sandbox_mktg.ab_acq_channel_attr
        WHERE channel = 'Free Offsite'
            and feature_name = 'Offsite - Free Search'
            and account_verified_date BETWEEN '20260208' and '20260214'
            and overall_status IN ('VERIFIED_POSITIVE','WHITELISTED')
            and is_active = 1
        group by 1
    ) pw ON c.Market_Country = pw.Market_Country
    LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT 
            CASE
                WHEN marketplace_id = '1' THEN 'US'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '7' THEN 'CA'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '3' THEN 'UK'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '4' THEN 'DE'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '5' THEN 'FR'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '35691' THEN 'IT'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '44551' THEN 'ES'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '6' THEN 'JP'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '771770' THEN 'MX'
                ELSE 'No Country Found'
            END AS Market_Country,
            count(distinct business_account_id) as previous_year_count
        FROM abbd_sandbox_mktg.ab_acq_channel_attr
        WHERE channel = 'Free Offsite'
            and feature_name = 'Offsite - Free Search'
            and account_verified_date BETWEEN '20250216' and '20250222'
            and overall_status IN ('VERIFIED_POSITIVE','WHITELISTED')
            and is_active = 1
        group by 1
    ) py ON c.Market_Country = py.Market_Country
),

eu_results AS (
    SELECT 
        'EU' as Market_Country,
        SUM(current_count) as current_count,
        SUM(previous_week_count) as previous_week_count,
        SUM(previous_year_count) as previous_year_count,
        CASE 
            WHEN SUM(previous_week_count) = 0 THEN NULL
            ELSE ROUND(((SUM(current_count) - SUM(previous_week_count))::FLOAT / SUM(previous_week_count) * 100), 2)
        END as "WoW",
        CASE 
            WHEN SUM(previous_year_count) = 0 THEN NULL
            ELSE ROUND(((SUM(current_count) - SUM(previous_year_count))::FLOAT / SUM(previous_year_count) * 100), 2)
        END as "YoY"
    FROM base_results
    WHERE Market_Country IN ('DE', 'ES', 'FR', 'IT', 'UK')
)

-- First Tab: All marketplaces plus EU and Total
SELECT 
    Market_Country,
    current_count,
    previous_week_count,
    previous_year_count,
    "WoW",
    "YoY",
    0 as sort_order
FROM base_results
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    Market_Country,
    current_count,
    previous_week_count,
    previous_year_count,
    "WoW",
    "YoY",
    1 as sort_order
FROM eu_results
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    'Total' as Market_Country,
    SUM(current_count),
    SUM(previous_week_count),
    SUM(previous_year_count),
    CASE 
        WHEN SUM(previous_week_count) = 0 THEN NULL
        ELSE ROUND(((SUM(current_count) - SUM(previous_week_count))::FLOAT / SUM(previous_week_count) * 100), 2)
    END,
    CASE 
        WHEN SUM(previous_year_count) = 0 THEN NULL
        ELSE ROUND(((SUM(current_count) - SUM(previous_year_count))::FLOAT / SUM(previous_year_count) * 100), 2)
    END,
    2 as sort_order
FROM base_results
ORDER BY sort_order, Market_Country;

-- Second Tab: EU Breakdown only
SELECT 
    Market_Country,
    current_count,
    previous_week_count,
    previous_year_count,
    "WoW",
    "YoY",
    0 as sort_order
FROM base_results
WHERE Market_Country IN ('DE', 'ES', 'FR', 'IT', 'UK')
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    Market_Country,
    current_count,
    previous_week_count,
    previous_year_count,
    "WoW",
    "YoY",
    1 as sort_order
FROM eu_results
ORDER BY sort_order, Market_Country;

Yun - flip-flopping based on Stacey's feedback vs. Brandon's (IS bidding the latest example, because of Stacey's feedback about inefficiency).
- Also, I let the entire team know that I'd implement the IS bidding testing today, and later in slack she lays out the test and asks if I can implement today. 
- Asked me if I'm free for a call, and showed me something that could easily be slacked. (10 min phone call to talk about 1 finding)

1/30 - UK/FR bid strategies increased and increased again by Yun week 3 and 4, which led to increase in campaign CPCs. (CPAs above target) And then asks me to investigate 2 campaigns with increased cost, when it was across whole portfolios.

2/6 - Brand regs weren't pacing along with cost, and Yun asked me to look into changing from IS bidding to max conv. I was testing IS bidding vs. max clicks bidding to find a middle ground away from complete IS bidding. She asked me to test for a few more days and move to conv. based bidding if it doesn't turn around, even though I asked to do this as a legitimate test. (she based this off of 4 days of registration data pacing, 2 of them being weekend)

2/12 - I wrote WBR callout, included overview/highlight/lowlight, and Yun came in later and added a paragraph that essentially talked about the same points. (ticket out to investigate UK, and ie%CCP changes; positives and negatives) I deleted my part because it became repetitive with the addition of her text, and this makes me feel that I shouldn't even write these.

3/6 - Yun mentioned a few times the importance of adding to the change log when making substantive changes. After the meeting, I look in UK change log for the same day, and noticed Yun added negatives and paused keywords related to Amazon Affiliates, Dropshipping, and fba. These are terms that converted in our account in the past, whether we were bidding directly on them as keywords or not. She paused and added negatives saying that they had 0 ref_regs, even though these terms had only spent $100 and about 20 clicks.

3/7 - I found out that Yun has also been pausing keywords using the Adobe platform. These are keywords that I recently created or turned on because of the lack of NB conversions, such as 'bestellen auf rechnung' in DE and 'cosmetic salon furniture' in UK, both of which had conversions, but maybe didn't sound like something we should have on for Yun. I went through each change from the Adobe user and activated those that I was specifically testing for NB. (There wasn't even enough data for these decisions)
*I'm noticing she makes actions that are ad-hoc and seemingly harmless, but can go against strategy that I've already discussed. (I remove negatives like affiliates/fba, and she adds back in. I add keywords to get more NB, and she pauses before they've got enough data.)

3/12 - EU accounts are under pressure. Stacey asked for insight on UK/FR/ES focused on YoY with specific asks. Yun jumped in and wanted me to repeat those asks to make sure I understood.

3/18 - Syncs regularly go over 45 mins. She also has tendency of listing out possible outcomes for tasks, and what we can do for each, even if I've already mentioned the task/possible outcome. (I've basically let her know I have that understanding)
I was working on callout for each market in EU, as a solution to not having all the details each each, and Yun working on synthesizing the info. She asked me to do the EU callout, which would mean that I was creating the callout for EU (what I've been doing), and the callout for each market. I had to clarify this a couple times, and she agreed to do the synthesis/story for EU.
I find myself managing up a lot more now.

This has become more a doc on leadership of Stacey and Yun. Stacey has gone off the rails the past few weeks with Brandon not in the WBR meetings. 
    Consistently mentioning that I don't think at a high level.
    Mentioned a few separate times that I need to stop making changes to the accounts
    Asked me about my to-do list during a WBR team meeting; I talked through a few things like invoice tasks (leftover work from her tenure), ENG accounts (requesting audiences, reporting, callouts for other teams' plans; Events and ENG)
    Consistently micromanaging changes made to the accounts, which has dis-incentivized me from making changes, and even looking deeply into the accounts like I would normally do.
    Disproportionately asking me for granular reporting directly. I am being managed by both Stacey and Yun; but Yun's tasks seem to come from Stacey.
    All of the above done in public; not classy at all, and has destroyed my morale, my wanting to work for the team with intrinsic motivation, and my trust in her leadership. (Yun's weakness from pressure were born from ignorance, Stacey's weakness under pressure born not from ignorance.)

Free Search Callout for Yoav in PreWBR (due Monday EOD)
column12:
SQL for FS
WITH base_results AS (
    SELECT 
        c.Market_Country,
        c.current_count,
        pw.previous_week_count,
        py.previous_year_count,
        CASE 
            WHEN pw.previous_week_count = 0 THEN NULL
            ELSE ROUND(((c.current_count - pw.previous_week_count)::FLOAT / pw.previous_week_count * 100), 2)
        END as "WoW",
        CASE 
            WHEN py.previous_year_count = 0 THEN NULL
            ELSE ROUND(((c.current_count - py.previous_year_count)::FLOAT / py.previous_year_count * 100), 2)
        END as "YoY"
    FROM (
        SELECT 
            CASE
                WHEN marketplace_id = '1' THEN 'US'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '7' THEN 'CA'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '3' THEN 'UK'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '4' THEN 'DE'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '5' THEN 'FR'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '35691' THEN 'IT'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '44551' THEN 'ES'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '6' THEN 'JP'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '771770' THEN 'MX'
                ELSE 'No Country Found'
            END AS Market_Country,
            count(distinct business_account_id) as current_count
        FROM abbd_sandbox_mktg.ab_acq_channel_attr
        WHERE channel = 'Free Offsite'
            and feature_name = 'Offsite - Free Search'
            and account_verified_date BETWEEN '20260215' and '20260221'
            and overall_status IN ('VERIFIED_POSITIVE','WHITELISTED')
            and is_active = 1
        group by 1
    ) c
    LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT 
            CASE
                WHEN marketplace_id = '1' THEN 'US'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '7' THEN 'CA'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '3' THEN 'UK'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '4' THEN 'DE'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '5' THEN 'FR'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '35691' THEN 'IT'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '44551' THEN 'ES'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '6' THEN 'JP'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '771770' THEN 'MX'
                ELSE 'No Country Found'
            END AS Market_Country,
            count(distinct business_account_id) as previous_week_count
        FROM abbd_sandbox_mktg.ab_acq_channel_attr
        WHERE channel = 'Free Offsite'
            and feature_name = 'Offsite - Free Search'
            and account_verified_date BETWEEN '20260208' and '20260214'
            and overall_status IN ('VERIFIED_POSITIVE','WHITELISTED')
            and is_active = 1
        group by 1
    ) pw ON c.Market_Country = pw.Market_Country
    LEFT JOIN (
        SELECT 
            CASE
                WHEN marketplace_id = '1' THEN 'US'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '7' THEN 'CA'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '3' THEN 'UK'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '4' THEN 'DE'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '5' THEN 'FR'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '35691' THEN 'IT'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '44551' THEN 'ES'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '6' THEN 'JP'
                WHEN marketplace_id = '771770' THEN 'MX'
                ELSE 'No Country Found'
            END AS Market_Country,
            count(distinct business_account_id) as previous_year_count
        FROM abbd_sandbox_mktg.ab_acq_channel_attr
        WHERE channel = 'Free Offsite'
            and feature_name = 'Offsite - Free Search'
            and account_verified_date BETWEEN '20250216' and '20250222'
            and overall_status IN ('VERIFIED_POSITIVE','WHITELISTED')
            and is_active = 1
        group by 1
    ) py ON c.Market_Country = py.Market_Country
),

eu_results AS (
    SELECT 
        'EU' as Market_Country,
        SUM(current_count) as current_count,
        SUM(previous_week_count) as previous_week_count,
        SUM(previous_year_count) as previous_year_count,
        CASE 
            WHEN SUM(previous_week_count) = 0 THEN NULL
            ELSE ROUND(((SUM(current_count) - SUM(previous_week_count))::FLOAT / SUM(previous_week_count) * 100), 2)
        END as "WoW",
        CASE 
            WHEN SUM(previous_year_count) = 0 THEN NULL
            ELSE ROUND(((SUM(current_count) - SUM(previous_year_count))::FLOAT / SUM(previous_year_count) * 100), 2)
        END as "YoY"
    FROM base_results
    WHERE Market_Country IN ('DE', 'ES', 'FR', 'IT', 'UK')
)

-- First Tab: All marketplaces plus EU and Total
SELECT 
    Market_Country,
    current_count,
    previous_week_count,
    previous_year_count,
    "WoW",
    "YoY",
    0 as sort_order
FROM base_results
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    Market_Country,
    current_count,
    previous_week_count,
    previous_year_count,
    "WoW",
    "YoY",
    1 as sort_order
FROM eu_results
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    'Total' as Market_Country,
    SUM(current_count),
    SUM(previous_week_count),
    SUM(previous_year_count),
    CASE 
        WHEN SUM(previous_week_count) = 0 THEN NULL
        ELSE ROUND(((SUM(current_count) - SUM(previous_week_count))::FLOAT / SUM(previous_week_count) * 100), 2)
    END,
    CASE 
        WHEN SUM(previous_year_count) = 0 THEN NULL
        ELSE ROUND(((SUM(current_count) - SUM(previous_year_count))::FLOAT / SUM(previous_year_count) * 100), 2)
    END,
    2 as sort_order
FROM base_results
ORDER BY sort_order, Market_Country;

-- Second Tab: EU Breakdown only
SELECT 
    Market_Country,
    current_count,
    previous_week_count,
    previous_year_count,
    "WoW",
    "YoY",
    0 as sort_order
FROM base_results
WHERE Market_Country IN ('DE', 'ES', 'FR', 'IT', 'UK')
UNION ALL
SELECT 
    Market_Country,
    current_count,
    previous_week_count,
    previous_year_count,
    "WoW",
    "YoY",
    1 as sort_order
FROM eu_results
ORDER BY sort_order, Market_Country;
